context._comment: Placeholder identifiers. Replace every EXAMPLE/111111111111 value with real values from your AWS Organization, Region, and Landing Zone before deploying. Prefer keeping real values in a gitignored cdk.context.json or passing them with -c so this file stays free of account-specific data.
context._prefix_comment: resourcePrefix is the single knob that namespaces every resource name, SSM path, and tag. All SSM paths are derived from it in bin/app.ts; do not hardcode the prefix anywhere else.
context._cidr_comment: Foundation VPC CIDRs. Must not overlap. Mirror the CloudFormation EndpointVpcCidr/EgressVpcCidr (network-foundation.yaml) and VpcCidr (workload-foundation.yaml) parameters. Omit a key to use the stack default.
context._workload_account_comment: workloadAccountId is the single example workload account (dev) that WorkloadFoundationStack and WorkloadValidatorStack deploy into to demonstrate onboarding. Test/prod and all other accounts are onboarded at scale by WorkloadAssociationStackSetStack, a service-managed StackSet that targets the workload OU (not individual account IDs).
context._multienv_comment: Optional per-environment knobs. Deploy the workload-side stacks for test/prod with -c environment=test|prod (default dev). Each environment maps to its own workload account, service network, and VPC CIDR below; unset values fall back to the dev/single-account values.
context._ou_comment: All three OU ARNs (dev/test/prod) are INPUTS: VpcLatticeCoreStack creates three service networks and RAM-shares each to its OU, so all three are required. devServiceNetworkId is an OUTPUT of that stack, fed back in only for the dev onboarding StackSet (a cross-account value that cannot be a CDK reference); test/prod are not onboarded in this reference, so their IDs are not needed here.
